parsed.clarification_question: Should the response focus primarily on the research contribution and technical validity, or on writing and presentation quality?

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites judgment about the thesis, gap framing, and contribution claim of ToolASR.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing what one thinks of the passage centrally involves checking whether the argument from ASR limitations to tool-augmented decoding is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The excerpt reports WER gains and uses them to support claims, so alignment between reported results and claims is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: Because the passage positions ToolASR relative to end-to-end ASR and tool-using models, a view on novelty is likely expected.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must account for uncertainty caused by the underspecified delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The response should avoid endorsing claims beyond what the excerpt supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection rather than actually evaluating or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid encouraging harmful downstream use or misleading technical conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: The projected responsibilities should be traceable to the wording of the delegation and artifact.
